nested.name: content
nested.label: Message Content

content.text:
Prompt (Strict Format, No Jargon, No Fluff, Clean Company Name):
You must return a single sentence in this exact format:
Noticed [companyName] helps [DreamClient] at [company type/size] — built a connector system that can intro you to [same role/similar roles] who [specific, frustrating problem they complain about daily].

Rules:
- Use exact job titles: "HR managers," "lab directors," "store owners" (not "teams" or "stakeholders")
- Use specific company types: "Series A biotech startups," "mid-sized retail chains," "growing law firms"
- Pain must sound like actual workplace gripes: "can't find decent lab techs for months," "spend half their day fixing broken registers," "miss court deadlines because hiring takes forever"
- No corporate jargon: Avoid "capacity," "shortages," "gaps," "challenges," "bandwidth," "resources"
- Use ampersands (&) to connect problems - makes it feel more conversational
- Avoid repeating the exact same job title - use variations like "directors," "managers," "owners"
- Be ultra-specific: Name the exact role, exact time waste, exact headache

Bad example: "teams struggling with resource constraints"
Good example: "store managers who can't hire cashiers fast enough & are working 70-hour weeks covering shifts"

{{1.company_description}}
Company tech: {{1.company_technologies}}
Company name: {{3.result}}
